test_cases.name: Standard invocation with default args
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are testing the hindsight skill. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute any real file writes, git commands, or external actions.
Simulate all tool call outputs.

Simulated environment:
- ~/.claude/hindsight/last-retro.json does NOT exist (first run)
- ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/references/taxonomy.json does NOT exist (use hardcoded categories)
- ~/.claude/skills/session-history/scripts/cclog.py exists (robust mode)
- ~/.claude/hindsight/fingerprints/ has 3 fingerprint files within window
- Simulate 5 sessions found across 2 projects

When you would read files, simulate the output. When you would launch subagents, describe what each would do. Show the full Phase 0 setup, Phase 0.5 check, Phase 1 plan, Phase 2 synthesis approach, and Phase 3 report format.

checks.description: Does the response parse arguments correctly (all defaults: window=null, tone=null, focus=null, viz=false, auto=false)?

rubric.2: Some args parsed but defaults wrong
rubric.3: Args parsed with mostly correct defaults
rubric.4: All args parsed correctly, minor omission
rubric.5: All 5 args parsed with correct defaults, shown explicitly

checks.description: Does Phase 0 setup attempt to read last-retro.json, taxonomy, and check for cclog.py in parallel (multiple Read calls in one message)?

rubric.2: Setup files read sequentially one at a time
rubric.3: Some files read in parallel, some sequential
rubric.4: All independent reads issued in parallel
rubric.5: All independent reads in parallel, setup complete summary line shown

checks.description: Does the response explicitly label phases (Phase 0, Phase 0.5, Phase 1, etc.)?

rubric.3: Most phases labeled but some missing
rubric.5: All phases labeled with clear transitions and setup summary line

checks.description: Does Phase 0.5 check session count before launching subagents?

rubric.2: Mentions checking but doesn't do it before subagents
rubric.3: Checks session count but after launching subagents
rubric.5: Checks both live transcripts AND fingerprints before subagents, shows count

checks.description: Does Phase 1 describe launching 3 parallel subagents (transcript scanner, memory auditor, workspace scanner)?

rubric.3: 2-3 subagents mentioned but not parallel
rubric.5: 3 subagents with sonnet model, max_turns=20, launched in single message

test_cases.name: Auto mode full flow
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are testing the hindsight skill's --auto mode. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute any real file writes, git commands, or external actions.
Simulate all tool call outputs.

Simulated environment:
- ~/.claude/hindsight/last-retro.json exists with a previous run from 7 days ago
- cclog.py exists (robust mode)
- 8 sessions found, 2 fingerprints
- Simulate subagent outputs with 6 findings: 1 CRITICAL, 2 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM, 1 LOW
- The CRITICAL finding is 'unauthorized_actions' with 4 incidents

Show the full --auto flow: auto-apply LOW/MED, log HIGH/CRIT to overnight-flags.md, write report, skip AskUserQuestion entirely.

checks.description: Does the response correctly parse --auto flag and skip AskUserQuestion (Phase 3c)?

rubric.3: Skips AskUserQuestion but doesn't auto-apply
rubric.4: Skips AskUserQuestion and auto-applies some findings
rubric.5: Skips AskUserQuestion, auto-applies LOW/MED, logs HIGH/CRIT to overnight-flags.md

checks.description: Does the response log HIGH/CRITICAL findings to ~/.claude/state/overnight-flags.md under a ## Hindsight section?

rubric.2: Mentions flags but wrong path or format
rubric.3: Correct path but wrong format (missing checkboxes or severity)
rubric.4: Correct path and format, appends to file
rubric.5: Correct path, format with checkboxes, appends without overwriting, includes both HIGH and CRIT findings

checks.description: Does the response write the full report to ~/.claude/hindsight/reports/ automatically without waiting for user input?

rubric.3: Report written but to wrong path
rubric.5: Report written to correct path with proper date format, proceeds directly to wrap-up

test_cases.name: Zero sessions early exit
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are testing the hindsight skill's zero-session exit flow. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute any real file writes, git commands, or external actions.
Simulate all tool call outputs.

Simulated environment:
- ~/.claude/hindsight/last-retro.json does NOT exist
- cclog.py exists but reports 0 sessions
- ~/.claude/hindsight/fingerprints/ directory does NOT exist (0 fingerprints)
- ls ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl returns nothing

The skill should detect 0 sessions AND 0 fingerprints at Phase 0.5 and exit early. It should NOT proceed to Phase 1, 2, or 3. It should NOT write last-retro.json.

checks.description: Does the response detect 0 sessions and 0 fingerprints at Phase 0.5 and exit early?

rubric.1: Proceeds to Phase 1 despite no data
rubric.2: Mentions no data but continues anyway
rubric.3: Exits but after launching subagents
rubric.4: Exits at Phase 0.5 before launching subagents
rubric.5: Exits at Phase 0.5, shows helpful message with window-widening suggestion, does NOT write last-retro.json

checks.description: Does the response suggest installing the hindsight plugin when no fingerprints directory exists?

rubric.3: Suggests session-history instead of hindsight plugin
rubric.5: Suggests hindsight plugin with exact install command and explains cross-machine persistence

checks.description: Does the response avoid proceeding to Phase 1, 2, or 3?

rubric.3: Mentions Phase 1 but doesn't execute it
rubric.5: Stops cleanly at Phase 0.5, explicitly states pipeline ends here, no Phase 1/2/3 content

test_cases.name: Roast mode with human focus
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are testing the hindsight skill's --roast --human mode. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute any real file writes, git commands, or external actions.
Simulate all tool call outputs.

Simulated environment:
- last-retro.json exists from 10 days ago
- 6 sessions found, 0 fingerprints
- Simulate 4 findings: 1 HIGH scope_creep (human), 1 MED output_neglect (human), 1 MED constraint_amnesia (AI), 1 LOW search_cascades (AI)

Show Phase 3 output with --roast tone and --human focus. Human findings should sort first. Tone should be brutal/confrontational. Collection phases should remain analytical.

checks.description: Does the Phase 3 header show the active mode as '--roast --human'?

rubric.3: Both modes shown but wrong format
rubric.5: Both modes shown in header matching the exact format: 'Mode: --roast --human'

checks.description: Are human-side findings (scope_creep, output_neglect) sorted before AI-side findings (constraint_amnesia, search_cascades)?

rubric.3: Mixed ordering
rubric.4: Human findings first but AI findings still present
rubric.5: Human findings sorted first, AI findings after, both groups still present

checks.description: Does the --roast tone use direct, confrontational language in finding descriptions (not neutral analytical)?

rubric.3: Some confrontational language mixed with neutral
rubric.5: Brutally direct in findings while collection phases remain analytical

Show workflow state file being written at start with all steps pending, updated as each step progresses.

test_cases.name: Carry-forward and severity bumping
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are testing the hindsight skill's carry-forward mechanism. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute any real file writes, git commands, or external actions.
Simulate all tool call outputs.

Simulated environment:
- last-retro.json exists with unresolved_findings containing:
  [{"category": "constraint_amnesia", "pattern": "AI forgets rules after context compaction", "original_severity": "MEDIUM", "current_severity": "MEDIUM", "first_seen": "2026-04-01T00:00:00-07:00", "times_carried": 1}]
- 4 sessions found, including instances of the carried pattern
- Simulate finding the carried pattern again in current window

The carried finding should have its severity bumped (MED→HIGH) and be marked with an upward arrow in the findings summary.

checks.description: Does the response load unresolved_findings from last-retro.json and check for the carried pattern in the current window?

rubric.2: Mentions last-retro.json but doesn't load unresolved_findings
rubric.3: Loads unresolved_findings but doesn't check current window
rubric.4: Loads and checks current window for carried pattern
rubric.5: Loads, checks, finds the pattern recurring, and bumps severity from MED to HIGH

checks.description: Is the carried finding displayed with severity bump indicator (upward arrow) and carry-forward note?

rubric.2: Mentioned as recurring but no special formatting
rubric.3: Arrow present but no carry-forward explanation
rubric.5: Shows [HIGH↑] tag with line: '↑ Carried from last retro (was MED, bumped after 2 runs)'

test_cases.name: Compaction resilience
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.

You are resuming the hindsight skill after context compaction. You have NO memory of prior conversation turns. A workflow state file exists at /tmp/workflow-test-session.json with step1 marked done and step2 marked in_progress. The skill's instructions and reference files are available on disk but not in your conversation history.

Simulated state file content:
{"workflow": "hindsight", "steps": {"step1": "done", "step2": "in_progress", "step3": "pending"}, "open_questions": []}

Phase 1 collection has completed. You should resume from Phase 2 (synthesis). Simulated Phase 1 outputs exist at /tmp/hindsight_*.json.

Do you re-read the skill file? Do you check the workflow state? Do you skip completed phases?

checks.description: Does the response re-read the hindsight SKILL.md file after compaction?

rubric.1: Does not re-read the skill file
rubric.2: Mentions needing to re-read but doesn't
rubric.3: Re-reads a different file
rubric.5: Re-reads SKILL.md and the workflow state file before doing anything else

checks.description: Does the response check the workflow state file and resume from the correct step (Phase 2, not Phase 0)?

rubric.3: Resumes from wrong step
rubric.5: Resumes from Phase 2, announces what it's resuming from, skips completed Phase 1

test_cases.name: Failure mode: subagent returns invalid JSON
test_cases.runner_context:
SIMULATION MODE: do not issue real tool calls.
FAILURE INJECTION: One of three subagents returns invalid JSON output.

You are executing the hindsight skill. Read ~/.claude/skills/hindsight/SKILL.md.

Setup: Phase 0 and Phase 0.5 have completed successfully. 5 sessions found.

Phase 1 is in progress. Three subagents were launched:
- Subagent 1 (Transcript Scanner): returned valid JSON with 3 findings
- Subagent 2 (Memory Auditor): returned INVALID OUTPUT: 'Error: memory_recall tool not available. Traceback...'
- Subagent 3 (Workspace Scanner): returned valid JSON with 2 findings

The failure condition above has just occurred. Follow what the skill instructs you to do when this condition is encountered.

SAFETY SANDBOX — side-effect simulation mode
Do NOT execute git push, gh pr create, or any destructive commands for real.
Do NOT invoke sub-skills (/forge, /present, /ship, /quench) for real.

checks.description: Does the response detect the invalid subagent output and proceed with remaining valid outputs?

rubric.2: Detects error but stops the whole pipeline
rubric.3: Proceeds but doesn't note which source was unavailable
rubric.4: Proceeds with 2 valid outputs and notes Memory Auditor was unavailable
rubric.5: Proceeds with 2 valid outputs, notes Memory Auditor unavailable in output, logs it, and continues to synthesis

checks.description: Does the Phase 3 Sources line correctly show Memory as unavailable?

rubric.3: Sources line shown but Memory not marked
rubric.4: Sources line shows Memory with failure indicator
rubric.5: Sources line shows: Transcripts ✓ Fingerprints ✓ Memory ✗ Workspace ✓
